author: premadāsa
verses:
saptadvīpa dīpta kari’, śobhe navadvīpa⋅purī,
jāhe viśvambhara devarāja
tāhe tā̐’ra bhakta jata, tāhāte śrīvāsa khyāta,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅kīrtana jā̐’ra kāja

jaya jaya ṭhākura paṇḍita
jā̐’ra kṛpā-leśa mātra, haiyā gaura⋅premapātra,
anupama sakala carita

gaurāṅgera sevā vine, deva⋅devī nāhi jāne,
cāri bhāi dāsa⋅dāsī laiyā
satata kīrtana⋅raṅge, gaura⋅gaurabhakta⋅saṅge,
aharniśi prema-matta haiyā

jā̐’ra bhāryā śrī-mālinī, pativratā⋅śiromaṇi,
jā̐’re prabhu kahaye jananī
nityānanda rahe ghare, putra⋅sama sneha kare,
stana jhare, netre vahe pānī

kabhu vā īśvara⋅jñāne, nati kare śrī-caraṇe,
kabhu kole karaye lālana
prabhura nṛtya-bhaṅga lāgi, mṛta putraśoka⋅tyāgī,
śuni’ prabhu karaye rodana

bhrātṛsutā nārāyaṇī, vaiṣṇava-maṇḍale dhvani,
jā̐’ra putra vṛndāvana⋅dāsa
varṇiyā caitanya-līlā, tribhuvana uddhārilā,
premadāsa kare jā̐’ra āśa
en_translation:
Navadvīpa-purī is resplendent, making the seven islands of the world shine. There, Viśvambhara reigns as the King of the Gods. Among all His devotees, Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita is famous, and his only work is the congregational chanting of the Holy Names of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

All glories, all glories to Śrīla Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita! By even a drop of his mercy, one becomes a recipient of Lord Gaura's love. All his deeds are incomparable.

Except for the service of Lord Gaurāṅga, he knows no other gods or goddesses. Together with his four brothers and his servants, he is constantly immersed in the joy of kīrtana. In the association of Lord Gaura and His devotees, he remains intoxicated with divine love day and night.

His wife is Śrīmati Mālinī-devī, the crest jewel of chaste wives, whom the Lord calls "Mother." Lord Nityānanda stays in their house and she shows Him affection just like a son. Milk flows from her breasts and tears flow from her eyes out of motherly love.

Sometimes, knowing Him to be the Supreme Lord, she bows at His lotus feet. At other times, she nurtures Him on her lap. For the sake of the Lord's ecstatic dancing, she even abandoned the grief for her dead son. Hearing of this, the Lord wept.

His brother's daughter is Nārāyaṇī, who is renowned throughout the Vaiṣṇava community. Her son is Śrīla Vṛndāvana Dāsa Ṭhākura. By describing the pastimes of Lord Caitanya, he delivered the three worlds. Premadāsa places his hope in such a personality.
